name: Coffee House Chocolate Spoons
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a piece of waxed paper on a cookie sheet large enough to hold 20 to 24 plastic spoons.
2. Place semisweet chocolate pieces in a heavy saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the chocolate begins to melt. Immediately remove from heat; stir until smooth.
3. Dip spoons into chocolate, tapping the handle of the spoon against the side of the pan to remove excess chocolate. Place spoons on waxed paper; refrigerate for 30 minutes or until chocolate is set.
4. Place milk chocolate (or chocolate of your choice) in a heavy saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until chocolate begins to melt. Immediately remove from heat; stir until smooth.
5. Place the melted chocolate in a small, heavy-duty sealing bag. Using scissors, make a small cut in the corner of the bag.
6. Drizzle the chocolate on both sides of the covered spoons, making a decorative pattern (small dots, zig-zag lines, circles, etc.). The above 2 steps can be reversed if you like!
7. Refrigerate spoils for 30 minutes to allow chocolate to set. Wrap each spoon separately in clear or colored plastic wrap.
8. Use ribbon to tie off the plastic wrap. Store in a cool, dry place for 2 to 3 weeks until ready to give as gifts.
9. Place chocolate-covered spoons in a new coffee mug and give along with homemade muffins, scones, biscuits, cookies, etc.
